channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump’s demand that US aid workers return home sparks outrage in Washington and anxiety overseas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Frustration boiled over Wednesday among supporters of the United States' lead aid agency at a Washington rally, and anxious aid workers abroad scrambled to pack up households after the Trump administration abruptly pulled almost all agency staffers off the job and out of the field.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan has stunned the region. Here's a look at the serious obstacles it faces
item.description: President Donald Trump's stunning proposal to forcibly transfer hundreds of thousands of Palestinians out of the Gaza Strip and develop it as a tourist destination faces major obstacles.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi Arabia contradicts Trump: No ties to Israel without Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia reasserted its support for an independent state for Palestinians and warned it would not forge diplomatic relations with Israel after President Trump floated the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine wants partner countries to join in postwar development worth billions
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine wants to collaborate with partner countries on postwar projects worth billions of dollars not just in mining rare earth elements, but also in energy and construction sectors to help rebuild the country, Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East expert says Israel and the US are back on the same page
item.description: Scholars for Peace in the Middle East Executive Director Asaf Romirowsky spoke with Fox News Digital about how Trump's return to DC is already having an impact on the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fans boo US national anthem before NHL, NBA games in Canada amid Trump tariff back-and-forth
item.description: Fans booed the U.S. national anthem before NHL and NBA games held in Canada on Tuesday, just after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and President Trump came to an agreement to delay tariffs Trump had threatened.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UN agencies raise alarm over Pakistan's move to deport thousands of Afghans waiting to go to the US
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The U.N. refugees and migration agencies on Wednesday expressed their concern over Pakistan's decision to deport thousands of Afghan refugees awaiting relocation to the United States and elsewhere. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USAID is going away. Here's what it's been doing in South America
item.description: The dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development will deliver a major blow to efforts including humanitarian assistance in Colombia, conservation efforts in Brazil and coca eradication in Peru — South American countries that have been a priority for the support.
item.dc:creator: Fabiano Maisonnave, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South African president phones influential billionaire Musk after Trump's funding threat
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa spoke with Donald Trump's “influential” billionaire adviser Elon Musk a day after the new U.S. president promised to cut funding for South Africa over a land expropriation law, Ramaphosa's spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN chief sounds the alarm amid fears over possible DOGE-inspired cuts after Trump's order
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres sends a letter to U.N. staff as President Donald Trump makes sweeping changes to U.S. foreign aid and funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico says it will not allow US to send Mexican migrants to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Mexico told the the U.S. government it will not allow Mexican migrants to be sent to the Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, detention camp, the country's foreign minister said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside the operations that took captive 2 North Korean soldiers fighting Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Ukrainian soldiers captured two North Korean prisoners of war last month, it provided the first undeniable proof of Pyongyang’s direct involvement in the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia's president orders national oil company to cancel US venture over environmental concerns
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro on Tuesday ordered the state-run oil company Ecopetrol to cancel a joint venture with a U.S.-owned company that was expected to produce around 90,000 barrels of oil per day, citing environmental concerns.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Jewish population in West Bank keeps rising. Settlers hope Trump will accelerate growth
item.description: BEIT EL, West Bank (AP) — The Jewish population in the West Bank grew at twice the rate of the general Israeli population last year, according to an advocacy group that hopes the Trump administration will support policies that help accelerate the growth of settlements in the occupied territory.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House flags top USAID boondoggles under Elon Musk's microscope
item.description: The White House has released a list of USAID programs its considers wasteful and that it says abused taxpayer funds by backing programs that supported DEI initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As hundreds of quakes rattle Greek islands, a few brave tourists have Santorini to themselves
item.description: SANTORINI, Greece (AP) — The hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled Greek islands on the Aegean Sea are a “seismic swarm" and could continue for weeks before eventually diminishing, the Santorini mayor told The Associated Press on Tuesday — pausing to chuckle as new tremors shook items on his des
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze puts at risk Ukraine’s wartime help for frontline evacuees
item.description: PAVLOHRAD, Ukraine (AP) — In what used to be the concert hall in this town in eastern Ukraine, cots are arranged on stage. Instead of music, the room is filled with the muffled sobs of local people driven from their homes by fighting in the country’s almost three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First military flight lands in Guantanamo Bay with migrants deported from the US
item.description: The first U.S. military flight deporting migrants from the United States to Guantanamo Bay has landed in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Denmark PM repeats Greenland ‘not for sale,’ but would welcome more American troops on Arctic island
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said she would welcome President Donald Trump sending more U.S. troops to Greenland, which is "not for sale.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Robert Levinson disappearance: FBI releases posters seeking info on two Iranian intelligence officers
item.description: The FBI is seeking information on two senior Iranian intelligence officers involved in the 2007 abduction of American Robert Levinson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: PM declares 'worst mass shooting in Swedish history' after gunman kills 10 on school campus
item.description: Swedish police are responding to a school shooting in Orebro, Sweden, on Tuesday after reports said at least 10 people were killed
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France’s government survives a no-confidence vote and a budget is finally adopted
item.description: France’s government has survived a no-confidence vote and the country’s 2025 budget is finally adopted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El Salvador agrees to accept US deportees of any nationality following meeting with Rubio
item.description: El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele has offered to accept convicted criminals of any nationality the U.S. wants to deport and house them in his country's prison system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian teenager dies in devastating shark attack, nearly 100 yards from popular beach: report
item.description: An Australian teenager from near Brisbane died on Monday after being bitten by a shark nearly 100 yards from the beach, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Emergency crews deployed on Santorini as earthquake swarm worries Greek experts
item.description: More than 200 undersea earthquakes have been detected near the Greek island of Santorini over the past three days, worrying Greek experts and island residents alike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
